title: C Command Line Arguments
content:
### Command Line Arguments in C

Command line arguments are parameters passed to a program when it is executed from the command line.

### The `main()` Parameters

```c
int main(int argc, char *argv[])
// or
int main(int argc, char **argv)
```

### Parameters Explained

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `argc` | Argument count (number of arguments) |
| `argv` | Argument vector (array of strings) |
| `argv[0]` | Program name |
| `argv[1]` | First argument |
| `argv[argc-1]` | Last argument |
| `argv[argc]` | Always NULL |

### Example Usage

Run: `./program hello 42`

| Index | Value |
|-------|-------|
| `argv[0]` | `"./program"` |
| `argv[1]` | `"hello"` |
| `argv[2]` | `"42"` |
| `argc` | `3` |

### Converting Arguments

Arguments are passed as strings. Use conversion functions:

```c
int num = atoi(argv[1]);        // string to int
double val = atof(argv[1]);     // string to double
long num = strtol(argv[1], NULL, 10);  // safer conversion
```

---

> **Key Point:** `argc` is always at least 1 (the program name). Convert string arguments to numbers using `atoi()` or `strtol()` as needed.
